old_claim.note: Formal Logic (Syllogism) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Epicureanism remains scoped to Epicurus's school, ethics, and atomist/materialist physics; Aristotelian syllogistic logic is not source-supported as a direct prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Introduction and philosophy sections describe Epicureanism as Epicurus's atomist, materialist, modest-pleasure philosophy and do not identify Aristotelian syllogistic logic as an enabling dependency.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports philosophy and atomist/materialist physics lineage, not a direct formal-syllogism prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Formal logic remains a neighboring classical reasoning node, but keeping it here launders a broad intellectual-context edge into a prerequisite claim.

ontology_before: Epicureanism depended directly on Formal Logic (Syllogism), implying Aristotelian deductive logic enabled Epicurus's school.
ontology_after: Epicureanism depends on Philosophy and Classical Chemical Arts/atomist material traditions; formal syllogistic logic remains separate.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: epicureanism no longer directly depends on formal_logic_syllogism.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to Epicurean formal logic or canonic as a logic-system node.
A source explicitly shows Epicureanism required Aristotelian syllogistic logic as a direct prerequisite.
A dedicated epicurean_canonic node is added and needs a narrower logic prerequisite.
short_reason: Epicureanism's sourced lineage is philosophical and atomist, not Aristotelian syllogistic.
